hero:
# GWU · Teaching Assistant / Decision Making & Data Analytics

**September 2024 – December 2024 · Washington, DC, USA**

## Hero Overview

Supported a multi-section graduate analytics cohort through weekly student support sessions, applied probability/data-analytics instruction, and AI-assisted assignment evaluation.

The role combined teaching operations with practical NLP automation: building a Python, NLP, and BERT grading-automation pipeline supporting 400+ assignments (human-in-the-loop) while refining scoring consistency with faculty stakeholders.

points.evidence: Built a grading-automation pipeline supporting evaluation of 400+ assignments using Python, NLP, and BERT (human-in-the-loop).
Ran weekly 3-hour support sessions translating probabilistic and data-analytics concepts into applied examples.
Refined the grading algorithm with 3 faculty members to improve scoring consistency and plagiarism detection.
Organized review workflows around 400+ assignments, supporting an estimated ~60% reduction in evaluation time.
Improved plagiarism-detection support as part of the grading algorithm refinement process.

storyline:
# Storyline

## Context

The Decision Making & Data Analytics course needed scalable student support and consistent assignment evaluation across a multi-section graduate cohort. Manual grading and feedback workflows created pressure on turnaround time and scoring consistency.

## Workflow

The role moved from weekly student support sessions into assignment review, NLP-assisted grading automation, faculty calibration, and plagiarism-detection support. The goal was to preserve teaching quality while making evaluation more repeatable.

## Technical Implementation

- **Student support:** ran weekly 3-hour sessions translating probability and analytics concepts into applied examples.
- **Grading automation:** built a Python, NLP, and BERT workflow supporting evaluation of 400+ assignments (human-in-the-loop).
- **Scoring consistency:** refined the grading algorithm with 3 faculty members over the term.
- **Evaluation operations:** supported an estimated ~60% reduction in manual evaluation time and roughly 10 hours/week of faculty time saved.
- **Plagiarism detection:** improved review support through scoring and similarity-oriented checks.

## Outcomes

The work supported evaluation across 400+ assignments, an estimated ~60% reduction in grading time, roughly 10 hours/week of faculty time saved, and improved consistency of analytics-course assessment workflows.
